config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dengan mudah dikorbankan atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru perlindungan hak individu adalah batas penting agar kekuasaan tidak sewenang-wenang. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang tetap menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan keamanan tiap orang. Di pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang mampu menyeimbangkan keduanya dengan adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my U.S. perspective, that kind of wording makes me uneasy because it can be used to justify government overreach, mob pressure, or sacrificing unpopular people for a supposed greater good. I do think there are real cases where public safety and shared interests matter, but those limits should be narrow, accountable, and never erase basic rights like free speech, due process, and bodily autonomy. A healthy society protects individuals first, because once individual rights are treated as optional, everyone becomes vulnerable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Pengalaman di Indonesia membuat saya justru waspada, karena dalih “demi kepentingan bersama” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, entah oleh negara, mayoritas, atau tekanan sosial. Kepentingan publik memang penting, terutama untuk keamanan dan ketertiban, tetapi hak dasar seperti kebebasan berpendapat, proses hukum yang adil, dan martabat pribadi tidak boleh dianggap sekadar pengorbanan yang bisa dinegosiasikan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat adalah masyarakat yang melindungi individu, bukan yang mudah mengorbankan individu atas nama kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual in any broad or automatic sense. From a U.S. perspective, that phrase is too easy to abuse, because it can become a blank check for government power, social conformity, or punishing people who are unpopular. Public safety and shared needs do matter, but they have to be justified case by case, with real limits and accountability, not by treating individual rights as a secondary concern. If a society starts by saying the collective comes first, it usually ends up deciding whose rights are expendable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia frasa “demi kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan terhadap orang per orang. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh dijadikan alasan otomatis untuk menyingkirkan hak dasar seperti kebebasan, martabat, dan proses hukum yang adil. Justru kalau hak individu dianggap sekunder, yang paling rentan biasanya kelompok kecil, minoritas, atau orang yang tidak punya kuasa. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang berani melindungi individu bahkan saat ada tekanan untuk mengorbankannya atas nama kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that framing feels dangerous because it can quickly become a justification for government overreach, majoritarian pressure, or sacrificing unpopular people in the name of the common good. I do think there are narrow situations where public safety or emergency needs require temporary limits, but those limits should be strictly justified, transparent, and subject to real checks. If individual rights are treated as secondary, then the very people society claims to protect are the ones most likely to lose their freedom.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
